name: Dgraph
description: Horizontally scalable distributed GraphQL database with native graph backend, ACID transactions, and real-time subscriptions

license.comment: Returned to Apache 2.0 in 2018 after temporary license changes. v25 releases all enterprise features under Apache 2.0.

hosting-options.comment: Dgraph Cloud offers shared ($39.99/mo) and dedicated ($199/mo) plans. Serverless offering in private preview as of 2025.

initial-release.comment: v0.1 released December 2015; first production-ready v1.0.0 released December 2017

data-model.comment: Native graph database with GraphQL-first design

topology.comment: Uses Raft consensus with leader election per Alpha group. Dgraph Zero orchestrates cluster membership.

sharding.comment: Data sharded by predicates (edge types) across Alpha groups. Zero automatically balances data across groups.

replication-factor.comment: HA setup uses --replicas 3 flag for Dgraph Zero

multi-region.comment: Supports geo-distributed deployments across multiple data centers

cap-position.comment: Prioritizes consistency using Raft consensus. Provides linearizable reads and ACID transactions.

consistency-model.comment: Provides linearizable reads and consistent replication via Raft. Single-key transactions imply linearizability.

acid-transactions.comment: Distributed ACID transactions with snapshot isolation, Jepsen tested

transaction-scope.comment: Cluster-wide distributed transactions, not limited to single keys or partitions

isolation-levels.comment: Snapshot isolation (not serializable). Conflicts detected by write-write conflicts only.

distributed-transactions.comment: Lockless distributed ACID transactions using MVCC and Raft consensus

query-language.comment: Native GraphQL support plus DQL (Dgraph Query Language), an extended GraphQL variant

sql-compatibility.comment: No SQL interface; uses GraphQL and DQL

secondary-indexes.comment: Supports multiple index types: term, exact, hash, fulltext, regexp, trigram, geo, datetime

joins.comment: Native graph traversals serve as distributed joins across predicates/nodes

full-text-search.comment: Built-in full-text search using bleve, with support for multiple languages

max-cluster-size.comment: No specific max cluster size documented. Tested with 1.9 billion RDF triples (250GB).

write-throughput.comment: No standardized benchmark figures available. Performance varies significantly by predicate distribution.

read-latency-p99.comment: No standardized p99 latency figures available

auto-scaling.comment: Manual scaling; automatic data rebalancing when nodes join, but no auto-provisioning

hot-spot-handling.comment: Dgraph Zero monitors data balance and moves predicates to groups with lower disk usage

zero-downtime-upgrades.comment: Rolling upgrades are a feature request (accepted but not implemented). Currently requires blue-green deployment.

online-schema-changes.comment: Schema can be updated via /admin endpoint without downtime

point-in-time-recovery.comment: Supports point-in-time recovery through backup and restore functionality

backup-strategy.comment: Supports full and incremental backups to local storage or cloud

kubernetes-operator.comment: Official Helm charts available but no dedicated Kubernetes operator

rust-driver.comment: Community Rust client (dgraph-rs) with ~99 GitHub stars

java-driver.comment: Official Java client (dgraph4j), requires JDK 11+ for v24.0.0

c-cpp-driver.comment: No official C/C++ driver; gRPC interface available for custom integration

orm-support.comment: No traditional ORM support; uses native GraphQL/DQL query languages

open-source.comment: Apache 2.0 license. v25 releases all previously enterprise-only features as open source.

free-tier.comment: Dgraph Cloud offers a free plan with limited features

pricing-model.comment: Shared tier starts at $39.99/mo, Dedicated at $199/mo. Includes 5GB data transfer, $2/GB additional.

cost-estimate-small.comment: Estimate based on dedicated tier pricing; actual costs vary by usage

change-data-capture.comment: Built-in CDC streaming to Kafka or local files. Now included in Apache-licensed v25.

watch-subscribe.comment: GraphQL subscriptions via WebSocket using @withSubscription directive

triggers.comment: No native database-side triggers; use Lambda functions for custom logic

event-streaming.comment: CDC supports Kafka as a sink (dgraph-cdc topic)

stackoverflow-tags.comment: Unable to retrieve Stack Overflow tag count
